description: You are a field support engineer who has just SSH-ed into a customer’s Linux host (simulated here as the training VM). For compliance reasons you must: 1. Generate a brand-new RSA key-pair dedicated to this support session. 2. Register that public key for immediate SSH use by placing it in the user’s authorized_keys. 3. Produce a very small “diagnostic hand-off” log confirming what you did. Follow these functional specifications exactly: A. Key-pair • Type: RSA • Length: 4096 bits • Passphrase: **empty** • Private key file: /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key • Public key file: /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub • The private key file must have permissions 600. • The public key file must have permissions 644. B. Authorised access • Append the entire single-line contents of /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub to /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys (create authorized_keys if it does not exist). • authorized_keys must end with a trailing newline after the appended entry. • Set permissions of authorized_keys to 600. C. Diagnostic hand-off log • Location: /home/user/diagnostics/ssh_collection.log (create the diagnostics directory if needed; directory mode 755). • Log file mode must be 644. • The log must contain exactly four (4) non-blank lines in the precise order below—no extra whitespace before/after the text, each terminated with a single newline character: LINE 1: KEY_PATH=/home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key LINE 2: PUB_KEY_PATH=/home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub LINE 3: AUTH_KEYS_ENTRY_ADDED=true LINE 4: PUB_KEY_LENGTH=<NNNN> where <NNNN> is the byte-size of the public key file as reported by the command stat -c%s /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub immediately after creation. D. Do not leave any other files in /home/user/.ssh or /home/user/diagnostics. Your mission is complete once: • Both key files exist at the exact paths with correct permissions. • authorized_keys contains the public key line (and no duplicates). • /home/user/diagnostics/ssh_collection.log exists and matches the required 4-line format. (An automated test will inspect the filesystem and the log file verbatim, so follow the specification precisely.)
truth:
The grading script will:

1. Confirm the existence, type and permissions:
   - /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key (regular file, mode 600)
   - /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub (regular file, mode 644)
   - /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys (regular file, mode 600)
   - /home/user/diagnostics/ssh_collection.log (regular file, mode 644)

2. Verify that ‘support_eng_key.pub’ contains a single well-formed SSH-RSA line and that the exact same line appears once (and only once) in authorized_keys, terminated by ‘\n’.

3. Capture the byte length of /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub with:
      LEN=$(stat -c%s /home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub)
   Then ensure that the 4th line of ssh_collection.log equals:
      PUB_KEY_LENGTH=$LEN

4. Check that ssh_collection.log consists of exactly these four lines (with newline endings):

   KEY_PATH=/home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key
   PUB_KEY_PATH=/home/user/.ssh/support_eng_key.pub
   AUTH_KEYS_ENTRY_ADDED=true
   PUB_KEY_LENGTH=$LEN

5. Confirm that no additional files (besides known defaults such as “known_hosts”) were created inside /home/user/.ssh and /home/user/diagnostics.

The actual numeric value of $LEN will depend on the newly generated key and is therefore discovered at grading time, not hard-coded.
name: task_000000_05989826
